LAW AND MOTION 301, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT JOSHUA W. KRIESEL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT -- 1ST C/A (NEGLIGENCE): OVERRULED. PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENCE. 2ND C/A (NEGLIGENCE): SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE -- DUPLICATIVE, 1ST C/A PLEADS NEGLIGENT ENTRUSTMENT. 3RD C/A (SLANDER): SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE -- NO FALSE STATEMENT MADE ABOUT PLAINTIFFS. 4TH C/A (FRAUD): SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE. PLAINTIFFS CANNOT PLEAD JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE. 5TH C/A (INTENTIONAL INFLICTION EMOTIONAL DISTRESS): SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE. MAINTAINING INNOCENCE TO THE POLICE DOES NOT SHOCK THE CONSCIENCE. 6TH C/A (NEGLIGENT INFLICTION EMOTIONAL DISTRESS): SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND PHYSICAL HARM DIRECTLY ARISING FROM DEFENDANT'S CLAIM OF INNOCENCE. Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Strike Punitive Damages On Behalf Of Defts
Off Calendar Mar-21-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Yvonne C. Penas, Esq. of PAVE & BOGAARDS 979-0480, for defendant Joshua W. Kriesel, moving to strike punitive damages

RULING:
No other appearance on this matter. Tentative ruling adopted -- DEFENDANT JOSHUA W. KRIESEL's Mtn To Strike Punitive Damages -- Moot. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (DEW/301/DR) Reporter: Candace Yount, #2737

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Yvonne C. Penas, Esq. of PAVE & BOGAARDS 979-0480, for defendant Joshua W. Kriesel, demurring party

RULING:
No other appearance on this matter. Tentative ruling adopted -- DEFENDANT JOSHUA W. KRIESEL's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT -- 1st c/a (Negligence): Overruled. Plaintiffs allege
facts sufficient to state a claim for negligence. 2nd c/a (Negligence): Sustained without leave -- duplicative, 1st c/a pleads negligent entrustment. 3rd c/a (Slander): Sustained without leave -- no false statement made about plaintiffs. 4th c/a (Fraud): Sustained without leave. Plaintiffs cannot plead justifiable reliance. 5th c/a (Intentional
Infliction Emotional Distress): Sustained without leave. Maintaining innocence to
the police does not shock the conscience. 6th c/a (Negligent Infliction Emotional
Distress): Sustained with leave to amend to plead emotional distress and physical harm directly arising from defendant's claim of innocence. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (DEW/301/DR) Reporter: Candace Yount, #2737
